frameworks.name: revealed preference theory
frameworks.one_line: Generalized Axiom of Revealed Preference quantifies economic rationality

frameworks.name: voxel-based morphometry
frameworks.one_line: Whole-brain gray matter density correlated with behavioral measure

frameworks.name: value-based decision making
frameworks.one_line: Economic choice as a window into neural valuation circuits

frameworks.name: Neurosynth meta-analytic coactivation
frameworks.one_line: Large-sample fMRI coactivation maps VLPFC to value regions

core_question: Which neuroanatomical changes in aging brains underlie loss of economic rationality?
key_assumptions: GARP violations are a valid index of economic irrationality
Gray matter density tracks functionally relevant tissue integrity
Cognitively normal elders (MMSE 26-30) reflect healthy aging
manipulations: Compared elders (>65y) versus younger adults on rationality
Regressed gray matter volume against GARP violation scores
Tested chronological age as a within-elder predictor
key_findings: Elders showed greater irrationality than younger subjects
Chronological age did not correlate with rationality within elders
VLPFC gray matter density correlated tightly with irrational behavior
VLPFC coactivates with nearly all value-associated regions in Neurosynth
interpretations: VLPFC integrity is a neuroanatomic locus for economic rationality in aging
Tissue loss rather than age per se drives decision-making decline
